title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Strep Throat
intro: Herbal teas for Strep throat are natural remedies made from various plant extracts used to alleviate symptoms associated with this bacterial infection. These teas can effectively treat Strep throat due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat the Streptococcus bacteria causing the infection. Examples of herbal teas used to treat Strep throat include Throat Coat tea, containing slippery elm and marshmallow root to soothe the throat, as well as Echinacea tea, known for its immune-boosting properties to combat the infection. Other herbal teas, such as Sage tea, Peppermint tea, and Ginger tea, are also used to reduce throat inflammation, ease pain, and promote relaxation during recovery.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been used traditionally to treat strep throat, a bacterial infection of the throat and tonsils. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, helping to reduce the severity of symptoms and alleviate the discomfort associated with strep throat. The flavonoids present in Echinacea purpurea teas, including kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to possess antibacterial properties, which may help combat the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria responsible for the infection. By incorporating Echinacea purpurea teas into a treatment regimen, individuals may experience reduced throat pain, swelling, and fever, allowing for a more rapid recovery from strep throat.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried flowers and put them in the tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers in the infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain and drink the tea, repeating as directed for up to 3 doses a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Echinacea purpurea teas may have potential for adjuvant symptomatic therapy in respiratory conditions, such as strep throat, among other conditions like the common cold, cough, and whooping cough.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat strep throat, but some possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat strep throat, it's essential to take precautions, such as monitoring your body's response, especially if you have allergies or are sensitive to herbal preparations, and to avoid consuming excessive amounts, as this may lead to adverse interactions with other medications or exacerbate existing health conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea per cup of boiling water.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage as needed with doctor's guidance.
Combine Echinacea purpurea tea with prescribed antibiotics if recommended by doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat strep throat due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and kill the streptococcal bacteria that cause the infection. By soothing the throat and reducing pain, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to alleviate the discomfort associated with strep throat, allowing the body to recover faster. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat strep throat include reduced pain, inflammation, and the risk of complications, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Take 2-3 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and place it in a teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day to help soothe a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas showed antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, the most common cause of acute pharyngitis, with a minimal inhibitory concentration (MIC) value of 39 μg/ml and a minimal bactericidal concentration (MBC) value of 78 μg/ml.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat strep throat, but it may cause side effects such as sodium retention, water retention, and an increase in blood pressure due to its high glycyrrhetic acid content. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is recommended to consume it in moderation and to avoid using it for prolonged periods, as well as to monitor blood pressure and potassium levels closely.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of warm Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root 3 times a day.
Use this treatment for 5-7 days for best results.
Combine with other natural remedies for faster relief.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas have been used to treat strep throat ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe the throat and reduce the severity of symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit antibacterial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria responsible for strep throat. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat, while its expectorant properties facilitate the removal of mucus, thereby aiding in the recovery process. Overall, Zingiber officinale teas are a beneficial herbal preparation for treating strep throat ailment due to their ability to combat the underlying bacterial infection and alleviate symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of sliced fresh ginger (Zingiber officinale).
Boil the water in a pot and add the sliced ginger. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the ginger slices. Let the liquid cool down.
Add honey to taste (optional). You can also add lemon juice for extra flavor.
Drink the ginger tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe a sore throat and reduce strep throat symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for strep throat may be beneficial due to the presence of lariciresinol, a compound that inhibits efflux pumps and enhances the antimicrobial potency of berberine, potentially leading to effective killing of drug-resistant bacteria at lower minimum inhibitory concentrations.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to potentially alleviate strep throat symptoms, but consuming it may cause side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset due to its spicy properties. To minimize potential discomfort, take the herbal preparation in moderation, stay hydrated, and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach to minimize stomach upset and potential allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger juice 2 times a day.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to tea for soothing effect.
Consume Zingiber officinale tea for 5-7 days to see improvement.
Combine Zingiber officinale tea with warm water for easy consumption.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat strep throat due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in thyme tea, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial infections, including Group A streptococcus, which causes strep throat. These bioactive constituents exhibit their antibacterial effects by disrupting the bacterial cell membrane and inhibiting the production of toxins, ultimately reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat strep throat include reduced inflammation, pain relief, and a shorter recovery time, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3-4 times a day to help soothe strep throat symptoms.
Repeat the process as needed, but consult a doctor before using Thymus vulgaris tea as a replacement for medical treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Wiadomosci lekarskie (Warsaw, Poland : 1960)", Thymus vulgaris teas may be beneficial for strep throat due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms and support the immune system.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Thymus vulgaris teas can be effective in treating strep throat due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties; however, possible side effects include mild digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and medications for diabetes and high blood pressure.

Precautions should be taken when using Thymus vulgaris teas, such as drinking it in moderation, avoiding its use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being cautious of its potential interactions with other herbal preparations and medications, and also monitoring blood sugar levels if taking it to treat strep throat.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 3-4 times a day.
Steep for 5-7 minutes with 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Use 1/2 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea as gargle 3-4 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris 2-3 times a day.
Continue for 7-10 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderberry teas, have been traditionally used to treat strep throat due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids present in Sambucus nigra teas help to reduce the severity of symptoms and prevent the spread of the infection. These bioactive constituents inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacterium responsible for strep throat, and also reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat. The benefits of using Sambucus nigra teas to treat strep throat include reduced symptoms, faster recovery time, and a natural alternative to antibiotics, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing this ailment.

Place the Sambucus nigra flowers in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to treat strep throat, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as hives or itching, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea or stomach upset. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications, as well as to monitor blood sugar levels and blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Sambucus nigra tea for strep throat treatment.
Use 2-3 cups of Sambucus nigra tea per day, divided into 3-4 servings.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Sambucus nigra extract per serving, 2-3 times a day.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day, with or without food, for 7-10 days.
Monitor symptoms and consult the doctor if there's no improvement within 5 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat strep throat due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce the severity of symptoms such as sore throat and fever, making it easier to recover from the infection. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea angustifolia teas, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the body's natural defenses against strep throat. By using Echinacea angustifolia teas, individuals can benefit from reduced recovery time, alleviated symptoms, and a lower risk of complications from strep throat.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea angustifolia roots. You can buy them at a health food store.
Rinse the roots with water and chop them into small pieces.
Use 1 tablespoon of the chopped roots and put them in a pot with 2 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink it as a tea. You can add honey if you want it sweet.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Journal of Family Practice", Echinacea angustifolia teas may be beneficial for the early treatment of acute upper respiratory infections, including strep throat, although the evidence suggests a need for more research on its effectiveness and optimal dosing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can help alleviate the symptoms of strep throat, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications. To use Echinacea angustifolia teas safely, be cautious with its preparation and consumption, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding, as excessive consumption may stimulate the uterus or affect milk production.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea angustifolia teas for strep throat.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea angustifolia root per 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 3-4 times a day.
Continue using the tea for 7-10 days to see improvement in symptoms.
Monitor your temperature and throat condition, and consult your doctor if no improvement.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat strep throat, a bacterial infection caused by Group A Streptococcus. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of these teas help to reduce the severity of symptoms and combat the infection. The bioactive constituents, including tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils, have been shown to possess antibacterial and antiviral properties, making them effective in treating strep throat. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat strep throat include reduced pain and inflammation, shortened recovery time, and a lower risk of complications, making it a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves.
Measure 1/4 cup of the gathered leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup or mug.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the leaves.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day to help soothe and reduce symptoms of strep throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology, Salvia officinalis teas may be beneficial for alleviating symptoms of oral mucositis, including those associated with strep throat.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat strep throat, but they may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Salvia officinalis teas, including monitoring blood sugar levels and blood pressure, and avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to limited research on its effects on the fetus and baby.

preparations.preparation_study: According to American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists, Ginkgo biloba teas have been identified as a potentially safe herbal therapy, with clinical trials suggesting efficacy for circulatory disturbances and dementia, but there is no specific information provided about its effectiveness for strep throat.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used as a complementary treatment for strep throat, but possible side effects include headaches, dizziness, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. However, when using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat strep throat, it is essential to be cautious and avoid excessive consumption, as it may interact with other medications and worsen symptoms in people with bleeding disorders or taking anticoagulant medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat strep throat due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the bacterial infection and reduce the severity of symptoms such as swelling and pain. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents like quinine and alkaloids that exhibit antibacterial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, the causative agent of strep throat. These bioactive constituents help to reduce the replication of the bacteria, thereby alleviating the symptoms and promoting recovery. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat strep throat include reduced inflammation, faster recovery, and fewer complications, making it a valuable natural remedy for this common illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the bark.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day to help soothe strep throat symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat strep throat, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to the high concentration of quinine, a compound that may not be suitable for everyone. When using Cinchona officinalis teas, precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, as it can lead to quinine toxicity, which may result in symptoms such as headaches, muscle weakness, and in severe cases, heart problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Trifolium pratense teas, also known as red clover teas, have been used traditionally to help alleviate the symptoms of strep throat due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation contains isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antibacterial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection causing strep throat. The bioactive constituents of Trifolium pratense teas, including saponins and flavonoids, contribute to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, ultimately helping to alleviate the symptoms of strep throat. Regular consumption of Trifolium pratense teas may also provide additional benefits, such as reducing the severity and duration of the illness, and promoting overall immune system health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Trifolium pratense flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired. Stir well.
Drink the tea 3 times a day to help soothe strep throat symptoms.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Trifolium pratense teas can be used to treat strep throat, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using Trifolium pratense teas include being cautious with the dosage, avoiding consumption with other herbal remedies, and monitoring your body for any adverse reactions, especially if you have a history of allergies or are taking prescription medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat strep throat due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of symptoms and combat the underlying infection. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus, including saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, work synergistically to modulate the immune system and alleviate symptoms such as sore throat, fever, and fatigue. By enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms, Astragalus membranaceus teas help to shorten the duration of illness and reduce the risk of complications. The benefits of using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat strep throat include reduced reliance on antibiotics, fewer side effects, and a faster recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of Astragalus membranaceus dried roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried roots in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried roots.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate strep throat symptoms.

Precautions to take when using Astragalus membranaceus teas for strep throat treatment include being aware of potential interactions with other herbal preparations and monitoring blood sugar levels if you have diabetes, and also being cautious when taking it with other medications that have a sedative effect.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been used as a natural remedy to treat strep throat due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and combat the bacterial infection that causes strep throat. By using Taraxacum officinale teas, individuals can help alleviate symptoms such as sore throat, fever, and swollen lymph nodes, ultimately promoting a faster recovery. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat strep throat include reduced risk of antibiotics resistance, fewer side effects, and improved overall immune function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers. Wash them with clean water.
Heat 2 cups of water in a pot. Bring it to a boil.
Add 1 tablespoon of Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Drink the tea 3 times a day to help soothe strep throat symptoms.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat strep throat, although possible side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners or diabetes medications. Precautions to take include monitoring blood pressure and sugar levels, as well as avoiding use in pregnancy or breastfeeding, as Taraxacum officinale may stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development.
